Class SongEditDialog

java.lang.Object
com.sebastiandorata.musicdashboard.presentation.libraryViews.SongEditDialog
public class SongEditDialog extends Object
A modal dialog that allows the user to edit the metadata of a Song.

Editable fields include title, album, artist(s), genre(s), and track number. Multiple artists and genres can be entered as a comma-separated list. When the user confirms:

  • Existing albums, artists, and genres are reused by name lookup to avoid duplicates; new entries are created and persisted automatically.
  • The updated song is saved via SongRepository.
  • The supplied onSaved callback is invoked so the calling view can refresh its list.

The dialog is application-modal and blocks the parent window until dismissed.

  • Constructor Details

    • SongEditDialog

      public SongEditDialog(SongRepository songRepository, AlbumRepository albumRepository, ArtistRepository artistRepository, GenreRepository genreRepository)
      Constructs a SongEditDialog with the repositories needed to look up and persist metadata entities.
      Parameters:
      songRepository - repository for persisting the edited song
      albumRepository - repository for looking up or creating albums by title
      artistRepository - repository for looking up or creating artists by name
      genreRepository - repository for looking up or creating genres by name

  • Method Details

    • show

      public void show(Song song, Runnable onSaved)
      Opens the edit dialog for the given song and saves changes on confirmation.

      Pre-populates all fields from the song's current state. If the user presses Cancel or closes the dialog, no changes are persisted.

      Parameters:
      song - the Song to edit; mutated in-place on save
      onSaved - a Runnable invoked after a successful save, intended for triggering a UI refresh; may be null
